behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names both files (ReactFlightReplyClient.js, ReactFlightClient.js) and describes the behaviors tested: encodeReply with JSX root + TemporaryReferenceSet, frozen elements + _debugInfo via Object.defineProperty.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes symptoms (throws, TypeError on frozen element) without prescribing specific code changes or naming Object.isFrozen.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_frozen_guard_in_add_async_info asserts the literal string 'Object.isFrozen' within 40 lines of 'function addAsyncInfo', forcing the specific guard implementation rather than allowing alternative frozen-element handling (e.g., try/catch, Object.isExtensible).

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests inject Jest cases and run yarn test, plus run repo lint/flow/tests; behavior is executed, not just grepped.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests actually exercise encodeReply and frozen client reference rendering; stubs would fail the Jest expectations.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Both behaviors mentioned in instruction (JSX root temp ref, frozen element debug info) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, repo_tests) and descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code bug fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers match the React repo structure.
